evidence: CollocateQueryHelper phases (collectHits, scoreHits, buildAndRankCollocates) are well-extracted private helpers with clear single responsibilities
BlackLabSnippetParser is a focused utility class: every method has a single concern, guard clauses appear at entry points only, no defensive sprawl inside logic bodies
HttpApiUtils methods are proportionate and focused; request-body reading, param parsing, and response writing are each in appropriately-sized helpers

SemanticFieldExplorer phase helpers (fetchSeedCollocates, buildNounToCollocatesMap, filterByMinShared, identifyCoreCollocates) are cleanly extracted, but the inline seedCollocateScores/seedCollocateFrequencies map-building loop in exploreByPattern is not extracted, creating an asymmetry with those named phases

ConlluConverter passes mutable state via a long[] array with named int indices (STATE_SENTENCES=0, STATE_TOKENS=1, STATE_CHUNKS=2) across method calls — an unusual idiom that relies on array-side-effect mutation rather than a simple mutable record

rotateChunkIfNeeded in ConlluConverter returns Optional<Long> as a rotation sentinel (present = rotated, value = new chunk count; empty = no rotation), with the caller responsible for resetting sentencesInChunk — indirect compared to a plain boolean or a small result record

MultiSeedExplorer.buildCoreCollocates re-streams seedCollocateMap.values() to compute avgLd for each common collocate, when avgLd could be accumulated during the earlier seedCollocScores pass in findCollocateIntersection

VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial is a flat inline method mixing validation, JSON parsing, and item collection; the sibling CorpusQueryHandlers uses a private BcqlRequest record + parseBcqlRequest helper for an equivalent concern, so the extraction style is inconsistent across handlers

strengths: Phase-labelled helper extraction in CollocateQueryHelper and SemanticFieldExplorer makes multi-step algorithms easy to trace without monolithic methods

BlackLabSnippetParser is a model of focused design: each method does exactly one thing, guard clauses appear at entry boundaries only, and the private extractLastLemma consolidation eliminates duplication
HttpApiUtils helper methods are proportionate: requireParam, parseIntParam, parseDoubleParam, and readBodyWithSizeLimit are each self-contained, with no defensive sprawl inside callers
GrammarConfigLoader uses small private helpers (parseAndValidateVersion, validateNoLegacyKeys, parseRelations, parseRelation) that keep the public load() entry point free of parsing detail
RelationPatternBuilder is a pure static utility class with no state mutation and no hidden side effects — every method is a transparent function of its arguments
low_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are isolated to a few specific classes and are pattern-level inconsistencies rather than systemic problems: unusual mutable-state idioms in ConlluConverter, asymmetric phase extraction in SemanticFieldExplorer, and inconsistent inline-vs-record parsing style in VisualizationHandlers relative to its sibling handlers.
low_level_elegance.score_rationale: The majority of the codebase is clean at the function level — proportionate helpers, bounded side effects, and precise edge-case handling are the norm across CollocateQueryHelper, BlackLabSnippetParser, HttpApiUtils, and the config layer. The codebase sits solidly above the 'significant drag' threshold (60) and below 'solid but uneven' (80) because a developer would mostly be well-served but would encounter a handful of moments of friction: the long[] state array in ConlluConverter is an indirect mutation pattern that breaks the otherwise clean style, the Optional<Long> rotation sentinel in the same class is unexpectedly indirect, and the inconsistent extraction style between VisualizationHandlers and its sibling CorpusQueryHandlers creates a 'why is this different?' moment. These are isolated rough edges, not deep structural problems, placing the score in the high-70s.

issues.summary: ConlluConverter threads mutable state via long[] array with named index constants instead of a named mutable record
evidence: private static final int STATE_SENTENCES = 0; STATE_TOKENS = 1; STATE_CHUNKS = 2; — three int constants used to index a long[] passed across processLines and rotateChunkIfNeeded
state[STATE_SENTENCES]++; state[STATE_CHUNKS] = rotation.get(); — mutation via array index inside processLines, with the actual semantics only apparent by cross-referencing the index constant
issues.suggestion: Replace the long[] state array with a private static final class ConversionState { long sentences, tokens, chunks; } mutable object. Pass and mutate it directly in processLines and rotateChunkIfNeeded. This makes mutation explicit and co-located with the fields it affects, and removes the need for the three index constants.

issues.summary: rotateChunkIfNeeded returns Optional<Long> as a rotation/no-rotation sentinel, an indirect convention that obscures its intent
evidence: private static Optional<Long> rotateChunkIfNeeded(...) { if (...) return Optional.empty(); writer.close(); return Optional.of(chunks + 1); } — Optional is used as a discriminated return, not as a nullable value

Caller: if (rotation.isPresent()) { writer = null; state[STATE_CHUNKS] = rotation.get(); sentencesInChunk = 0; } — the three-statement block on the Optional.isPresent() path is non-obvious; caller must know the value is the new chunk count
issues.suggestion: Replace with a boolean return: if rotation occurred, close the writer and increment the chunk counter inside rotateChunkIfNeeded (or in a dedicated rotate() helper that also resets state), and return true. The caller's if-block shrinks to a single flag check. Alternatively, inline the rotation logic into processLines directly since rotateChunkIfNeeded is only three lines.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern builds seedCollocateScores/seedCollocateFrequencies inline in the main method body while all other phases are extracted to named helpers
evidence: Map<String, Double> seedCollocateScores = new LinkedHashMap<>(); Map<String, Long> seedCollocateFrequencies = new LinkedHashMap<>(); for (QueryResults.WordSketchResult r : seedRelations) { ... } — this phase 1b map-building block sits inline in exploreByPattern alongside fetchSeedCollocates, buildNounToCollocatesMap, filterByMinShared, identifyCoreCollocates which are all extracted methods
The four extracted phase helpers each have their own Javadoc phase comment; the inline map loop does not follow this convention
issues.suggestion: Extract the inline map-building loop into a private helper, e.g. private Map<String, Double> buildSeedCollocateScoreMap(List<WordSketchResult> results) and private Map<String, Long> buildSeedCollocateFrequencyMap(List<WordSketchResult> results), or a single private record SeedCollocateMaps containing both maps. This makes the main method body a uniform sequence of named-phase calls.

issues.summary: VisualizationHandlers.handleVisualRadial parses its POST body inline, while the sibling CorpusQueryHandlers uses a dedicated private record + parseBcqlRequest helper for the same concern
evidence: handleVisualRadial mixes readBodyWithSizeLimit, JSON.parseObject, null-coalescing (if (center == null) center = ""), int extraction, array iteration, and RenderMode validation all in one flat method body

CorpusQueryHandlers has a private record BcqlRequest(String query, int top) {} and a dedicated parseBcqlRequest(HttpExchange) method that throws IllegalArgumentException for validation failures — a clear pattern for the same POST-body parsing concern
issues.suggestion: Extract a private record RadialRequest(String center, int width, int height, List<RadialPlot.Item> items, String mode) and a private parseRadialRequest(HttpExchange) method analogous to CorpusQueryHandlers.parseBcqlRequest. Move all validation (RenderMode check, null-center coercion, item count clamping) into parseRadialRequest. This aligns VisualizationHandlers with the established pattern in its sibling handler.

root_causes: The long[] state pattern and Optional sentinel in ConlluConverter may reflect early prototyping style that was not revisited when the class was otherwise cleaned up
The helper extraction asymmetry in SemanticFieldExplorer and VisualizationHandlers suggests the extraction refactors were applied incrementally and never fully completed across all code paths
